title: Ideology and policymaking
description: Test your knowledge on how US political culture influences policymaking over time!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines policy mood?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how political scientists measure the public’s preferences toward policy choices
how people adopt political attitudes and values over their lifetimes
how organizations estimate public attitudes toward issues by surveying a small sample of the public
how the president can use their presidency to present an agenda directly to public

assessment_items.question_content:
| Position issue | Valence issue
- | - | -
**A**| Politicians should be honest about their actions. | The most important issue of the twenty-first century is climate change.
**B** | Every person should have the right to work and provide for their family. | The government should address corruption within itself.
**C**| The military should defend the United States from threats. | The government should not provide food stamps for low-income families.
**D** | The government should increase spending on public education. | Every person should have the right to a good education.

**Which of the following makes a correct comparison between position issues and valence issues?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Public’s policy priorities: 2011-2019** ||
Topic | Jan 2011 | Jan 2015 | Jan 2019
- | - | - | -
Strengthening nation’s economy | $87\%$ | $75\%$ | $70\%$
Protect environment | $40\%$ | $51\%$ | $56\%$
Improving job situation | $84\%$ | $67\%$| $50\%$
Reducing budget deficit | $64\%$ | $64\%$ | $48\%$
Dealing with climate change | $26\%$ | $34\%$ | $44\%$

**Which of the following statements is reflected in the data in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Public preferences toward policy priorities change over time.
Policy preferences rarely change since people’s ideology does not change.
Policy priorities are determined by a small, elite group.
Polls are the best way for politicians to gauge public opinion on policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Public’s policy priorities: 2011-2019** ||
Topic | Jan 2011 | Jan 2015 | Jan 2019
- | - | - | -
Strengthening nation’s economy | $87\%$ | $75\%$ | $70\%$
Protect environment | $40\%$ | $51\%$ | $56\%$
Improving job situation | $84\%$ | $67\%$| $50\%$
Reducing budget deficit | $64\%$ | $64\%$ | $48\%$
Dealing with climate change | $26\%$ | $34\%$ | $44\%$

**Based on the data shown in the table, which of the following statements is true about policy preferences?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Policy preferences reflect the attitudes and beliefs of Americans participating in politics.
Policy preferences reflect the ideology of the party holding the presidency.
Policy preferences do not change often because people maintain the same ideology throughout their lifetimes.
Policy preferences often skew conservative, prioritizing keeping the size of government small.

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Public’s policy priorities: 2011-2019** ||
Topic | Jan 2011 | Jan 2015 | Jan 2019
- | - | - | -
Strengthening nation’s economy | $87\%$ | $75\%$ | $70\%$
Protect environment | $40\%$ | $51\%$ | $56\%$
Improving job situation | $84\%$ | $67\%$| $50\%$
Reducing budget deficit | $64\%$ | $64\%$ | $48\%$
Dealing with climate change | $26\%$ | $34\%$ | $44\%$

**Based on the information in the table, which of the following is one likely implication of this public opinion poll on the policy agenda?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Members of Congress create environmental policies to address climate change and continue their work in improving the economy.
Environmental agencies focus on the public’s policy priorities and begin implementing environmental policy.
The president signs a series of executive orders to address the economic and environmental priorities listed in the table.
Major media organizations send out a poll asking people to decide which policy the government should work on first.

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Public’s policy priorities: 2011-2019** ||
Topic | Jan 2011 | Jan 2015 | Jan 2019
- | - | - | -
Strengthening nation’s economy | $87\%$ | $75\%$ | $70\%$
Protect environment | $40\%$ | $51\%$ | $56\%$
Improving job situation | $84\%$ | $67\%$| $50\%$
Reducing budget deficit | $64\%$ | $64\%$ | $48\%$
Dealing with climate change | $26\%$ | $34\%$ | $44\%$

**Which of the following statements best represents a reason that the public’s policy priorities changed from 2011 to 2019?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: New problems emerged, and the government addressed some problems, changing the public’s attitudes about what the government should work on next.
The government failed to help the nation recover from a major economic crisis from 2011 to 2019, causing public support for the government to solve economic problems to rise.
An environmental social movement emerged for the first time, drawing national attention to the issue of climate change.
The government solved all economic problems, allowing the public to devote its attention to environmental issues, like climate change.

assessment_items.question_content:
Following an uptick in juvenile crime, Star City institutes a curfew that prohibits any person under the age of 18 from being in a public place without adult supervision between the hours of 9:00pm and 5:00am.

**What does this scenario illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how the government tries to balance protecting personal liberty and promoting social order
how federalism distributes the powers of enforcing order across different levels of government
how public opinion about government can change how the government reacts to social and economic issues
how local agencies work with the federal bureaucracy to implement policies passed by Congress

assessment_items.question_content:
The city of Metropolis places cameras on major intersections to take pictures of license plates. However, the pictures capture more than license plates, sometimes capturing the faces of people involved in protest activity. 

**What does this scenario illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how new technology has forced the government to balance enforcing order with protecting civil liberties
how political attitudes about public safety policies shift as different parties come to power
how social movements pressure the government to address social issues through legislation
how the distribution of powers between different levels of government can impact the implementation of policy

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2019, New York Governor Andrew Cuomo signed a bill that ended the practice of vaccination exemptions based on religious beliefs. 

**What does this scenario illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how the government tries to balance promoting stability and safety and ensuring civil liberties
how the precedents of major Supreme Court cases have affected the interpretation of the Fourth Amendment
how globalization has impacted Americans’ political attitudes about the role of government
how state governments can refuse to implement federal policy and avoid the bureaucracy

assessment_items.question_content:
####Source 1:

> “As we consider whether to reauthorize parts of the PATRIOT Act, we must take stock of where we are today. It is no secret that NSA collects vast sums of information and at one point or another has collected information on virtually every person in this country who uses a telephone. That is no great secret. Since June 2013, we have learned that the NSA collects phone call metadata, including the numbers of both parties, location, time, and duration. They collect text messages, email chat, and Internet browsing history; smart phone app data, including Google Maps, which can pinpoint a person's location to within a few yards. They collect maps of people's social networks, bank and credit card transactions. This is just the tip of the iceberg. There is undoubtedly much more being done that we simply don't know anything about.”

>-Source: Senator Bernie Sanders, in a debate about the USA Freedom Act, 2015

####Source 2:

> “The collection of telephone metadata in bulk facilitates our mapping of terrorist networks and our ability to disrupt terrorist plots. Contrary to the wild fantasies that critics frequently spout, this collection does not meaningfully intrude on our privacy. It does not involve the NSA listening in on anyone's calls. It is simply a very important means of finding a proverbial needle in a haystack. We should reauthorize this authority without delay.”

>-Source: Senator Orrin Hatch, in a debate about the USA Freedom Act, 2015

**Which of the following scenarios illustrates the author’s concerns?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The House of Representatives impeaches a cabinet official because he makes multiple calls to an alcohol addiction hotline.
The National Security Agency can identify several members of a domestic terrorist group using their cell metadata.
The iPhone allows customers to open their phones using facial recognition software, and the company hosts all of that data.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation gets a warrant to listen in on the phone conversations of a suspected drug dealer.

**The debate shown in the two sources about the USA Freedom Act reflects which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the ways in which the government attempts to balance individual liberty and public order in policy
the lack of gridlock in Congress that makes it easier to pass laws through both chambers of Congress
the methods Congress has to oversee the actions of bureaucratic agencies as they implement policies
the change in public attitudes about the role of government following the attack on September 11th

**Which of the following newspaper headlines best summarizes the excerpts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Senators debate how to protect the public without violating civil liberties
Senators overwhelmingly support increased efforts to stop terrorist threats
Senators divided on whether the government should tax new technology
Senators increase security agencies’ powers to monitor citizens’ lawful actions
